disease_site: Relapsed/Refractory Hodgkin Lymphoma or Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes; day 2 etoposide given before carboplatin
AUC 5 using Calvert formula (max 790mg); in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes; given after etoposide on day 2

5000mg/m² total given as two 2500mg/m² doses over 12h each (BID); mixed with Mesna 2500mg/m² per bag in sodium chloride 0.9%; total 24h continuous infusion over day 2; ifosfamide encephalopathy risk — have methylene blue available [VERIFIED: Ifosfamide 5000mg/m² over 24h is standard in salvage R-ICE protocol — higher than standard ifosfamide dosing]
2500mg/m² mixed with each ifosfamide bag (BID, total 5000mg/m² over day 2); uroprotection for ifosfamide
3000mg/m² over 12 hours on day 3; continued uroprotection after ifosfamide

dose_modifications.notes: Proceed at full dose
Delay cycle until neutrophils >=1.0 AND platelets >=75; cycle length is recovery-based
Discuss with consultant; ifosfamide renally excreted — consider dose adjustment or alternative
Stop ifosfamide immediately; administer methylene blue 50mg IV as treatment; methylene blue 50mg IV three times daily as prophylaxis for subsequent cycles if rechallenge considered

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 1-3)
Dexamethasone 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 1-3)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily when required
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily (cycle 1 only, for tumour lysis prophylaxis)
Appropriate IV hydration per local protocol
Methylene blue 50mg IV available at all times during ifosfamide infusion (for encephalopathy)
special_warnings: Hodgkin Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD; ONLY irradiated blood products must be used for life
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: monitor for confusion, somnolence, seizures; have methylene blue 50mg IV immediately available
Mesna uroprotection is mandatory with ifosfamide; monitor urine for haematuria
Cycle length is variable and recovery-based; not fixed at 21 days
Carboplatin dose calculated by Calvert formula (AUC 5); maximum dose 790mg
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before each cycle for carboplatin dose calculation
Carboplatin: avoid aluminium-containing equipment

disease_site: Relapsed/Refractory Hodgkin Lymphoma or Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

drugs.notes: 600mg/m² IV over 30 minutes before first ifosfamide dose on day 1; uroprotection pre-ifosfamide | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.
IV injection over 10 minutes on day 1; cumulative lifetime maximum 900mg/m²; LVEF >=50% required before starting; vesicant — check line patency before administration
In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes; administer after epirubicin on day 1
1500mg/m² BID each day (days 1-3); each 1500mg/m² dose mixed with Mesna 1500mg/m² in same bag; 24h continuous infusion each day; ifosfamide encephalopathy risk — have methylene blue available

1500mg/m² mixed with each ifosfamide bag (BID per day, total 3000mg/m²/day); uroprotection throughout ifosfamide infusion days 1-3 | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

1800mg/m² over 12 hours on day 4; continued uroprotection after completion of ifosfamide | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

dose_modifications.notes: Proceed at full dose
Delay until counts recover; discuss with consultant
Reduce to 50% dose
Omit epirubicin; discuss with consultant
Discuss with consultant; ifosfamide renally excreted — dose reduction or alternative may be required
Do not administer epirubicin; discuss with consultant

dose_modifications.condition: Cumulative epirubicin dose approaching or exceeding 900mg/m²
dose_modifications.notes: Lifetime maximum cumulative dose 900mg/m²; do not exceed
Stop ifosfamide immediately; administer methylene blue 50mg IV; consider methylene blue prophylaxis for subsequent cycles

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 1-3)
Dexamethasone 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 1-3)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily when required
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily (cycle 1 only)
Appropriate IV hydration per local protocol
Methylene blue 50mg IV available at all times during ifosfamide infusion
special_warnings: LVEF must be >=50% before commencing treatment; repeat LVEF assessment as clinically indicated
Cumulative lifetime maximum epirubicin dose is 900mg/m²; document and track cumulative anthracycline exposure
Epirubicin is a vesicant — ensure secure venous access and check line patency before administration
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: monitor for confusion, somnolence, agitation; have methylene blue 50mg IV available
Mesna uroprotection is mandatory with ifosfamide; monitor urine for haematuria
Hodgkin Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD; ONLY irradiated blood products must be used for life

disease_site: Relapsed/Refractory Hodgkin Lymphoma or Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma (high-dose conditioning for autologous PBSCT)

drugs.notes: Single oral dose on day -6; taken with anti-emetics; capsules should not be opened; give in divided doses if total dose >400mg to reduce nausea
200mg/m² every 12 hours (q12h) on days -5 to -2 (8 doses total); each dose in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes; total 8 infusions
200mg/m² once daily on days -5 to -2 (4 doses total); given as two sequential infusions of 100mg/m² each over 60 minutes (2h total per day); in sodium chloride 0.9%; administer after cytarabine
In sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on day -1; must be given within 60 minutes of reconstitution; administer via central line; highly emetogenic

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce melphalan to 75% (105mg/m²); discuss with transplant team
Discuss with consultant; hepatic impairment may affect lomustine metabolism

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 non-haematological toxicity from previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Conditioning regimen is single cycle; dose modifications not applicable in standard practice; discuss with transplant team

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg IV (before each dose of etoposide, cytarabine, and melphalan)
Aprepitant 125mg oral day -6, then 80mg oral days -5 to -1 (for lomustine and melphalan)
Dexamethasone 8mg IV (before highly emetogenic drugs)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (antiviral prophylaxis throughout conditioning)
Fluconazole 50mg oral once daily (antifungal prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (PCP prophylaxis)
Chlorhexidine mouthwash four times daily (mucositis prevention)
Adequate IV hydration before melphalan
Cryotherapy (ice chips) during melphalan infusion to reduce oral mucositis
special_warnings: This is a high-dose conditioning regimen for autologous peripheral blood stem cell transplant (PBSCT); autologous stem cells infused on day 0
Profound and prolonged myelosuppression is expected and intended; patient requires inpatient care throughout
Lomustine is highly emetogenic; anti-emetics must be given before administration
Melphalan must be administered via central venous access; peripheral administration not appropriate
Melphalan is unstable after reconstitution; must be infused within 60 minutes
Mucositis is common and can be severe; intensive oral hygiene and cryotherapy during melphalan are essential
Growth factors (G-CSF) given post-transplant per local HSCT protocol from day +1 or later
Day numbering uses negative convention: day -6 is first day of conditioning, day 0 is stem cell infusion day

NEGATIVE DAY NUMBERING: Day 0 = stem cell reinfusion day (ASCT). LEAM conditioning chemotherapy on days -6 to -1. All negative days are relative to transplant day. This is standard ASCT conditioning — intentional.

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.

disease_site: Primary CNS Lymphoma (DLBCL or other aggressive B-cell lymphoma with CNS involvement)

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%; administer according to local rituximab infusion guidelines; first infusion start at 50mg/h, escalate as tolerated

Loading dose 500mg/m² over 15 minutes on day 2, immediately followed by 3000mg/m² maintenance infusion over 3 hours; total methotrexate dose 3500mg/m²; urinary alkalinisation required (urine pH >7) before and throughout; CrCl >=50 ml/min required; folinic acid rescue must commence 24 hours after start of methotrexate infusion | LOADING DOSE (500mg/m²): two Methotrexate entries exist on day 2 — 500mg/m² loading infusion over 15 minutes followed immediately by 3000mg/m² maintenance infusion over 3 hours. Total day 2 dose = 3500mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.

Maintenance infusion 3000mg/m² over 180 minutes, given immediately after 500mg/m² loading dose; total methotrexate dose on day 2 is 3500mg/m²; monitor serum methotrexate levels every 24h from 24h after start until level <0.1 micromol/L; folinic acid rescue 30mg every 3 hours IV starting 24h after beginning of methotrexate infusion, continued until level <0.1 micromol/L | MAINTENANCE INFUSION DOSE (3000mg/m²): two Methotrexate entries exist on day 2 — 500mg/m² loading infusion over 15 minutes followed immediately by this 3000mg/m² maintenance infusion over 3 hours. Total day 2 dose = 3500mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.

30mg every 3 hours IV starting 24 hours after the beginning of methotrexate infusion; continue until serum methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L; may switch to oral from day 5 if tolerated; folinic acid rescue is mandatory

2000mg/m² every 12 hours on days 3-4 (4 doses total); each dose in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 3 hours; total 4 infusions; ocular toxicity risk — steroid eye drops required during and 48h after cytarabine
In 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on day 5; use non-PVC giving set

dose_modifications.notes: Do not administer methotrexate if CrCl <50 ml/min; discuss with consultant regarding alternative treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Methotrexate level >1.0 micromol/L at 48h after start of infusion
dose_modifications.notes: Continue folinic acid rescue at increased frequency; consider glucarpidase; discuss urgently with consultant
Delay methotrexate until urine pH >=7; continue urinary alkalinisation with IV sodium bicarbonate
Delay cycle by up to 1 week; discuss with consultant if counts do not recover
Omit methotrexate; discuss urgently with CNS lymphoma specialist

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 2-5)
Dexamethasone 8mg IV (30 min before chemotherapy, days 2-5)
Urinary alkalinisation: sodium bicarbonate IV to maintain urine pH >7 before and throughout methotrexate (day 2)
Adequate IV hydration (>3 litres/m²/day) before, during and after methotrexate
Steroid eye drops (e.g. dexamethasone 0.1%) during cytarabine and for 48h after (days 3-6)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (antiviral prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (PCP prophylaxis)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily (cycle 1 only, tumour lysis prophylaxis)
special_warnings: CrCl >=50 ml/min is mandatory before each cycle of methotrexate; check EDTA GFR or calculated CrCl
Urinary alkalinisation (pH >7) is required before and throughout methotrexate infusion to prevent nephrotoxicity
Serum methotrexate levels must be monitored every 24 hours from 24h after start of infusion until <0.1 micromol/L
Folinic acid rescue (30mg every 3h IV) is mandatory and must begin 24h after the start of methotrexate
Delayed methotrexate clearance requires prolonged folinic acid rescue; glucarpidase may be required
High-dose cytarabine causes ocular toxicity (conjunctivitis, keratitis); steroid eye drops mandatory during and 48h after
This protocol requires inpatient administration; methotrexate cannot be given until urine pH >7
Rituximab infusion reactions: have resuscitation equipment and adrenaline immediately available

Anti-CD20 therapy: HBV reactivation risk — screen all patients for HBsAg and HBcAb before starting. HBsAg positive: antiviral prophylaxis mandatory (entecavir or tenofovir). HBcAb positive/HBsAg negative: prophylaxis or close monitoring per local hepatology policy.

disease_site: Primary CNS Lymphoma or CNS Prophylaxis in aggressive lymphoma

drugs.notes: Default dose 3000mg/m² (range 3000-6000mg/m² per consultant decision); in sodium chloride 0.9% over 240 minutes; urinary alkalinisation required (urine pH >7) before starting; CrCl >=50 ml/min required; methotrexate levels monitored every 24h from 24h after start until <0.1 micromol/L; cycle length is variable and recovery-based (typically 14-21 days)

30mg every 3 hours IV starting 24 hours after the start of methotrexate infusion; continue until serum methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L; may be switched to oral folinic acid from day 5 if tolerated and levels falling appropriately; folinic acid rescue is mandatory

dose_modifications.notes: Do not administer; CrCl >=50 ml/min is mandatory; discuss with consultant
Delay until urine pH >=7; continue urinary alkalinisation

dose_modifications.condition: Methotrexate level >1.0 micromol/L at 48h after start
dose_modifications.notes: Intensify folinic acid rescue; consider glucarpidase; urgent senior review

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin >50 micromol/L or ALT/AST >5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Omit methotrexate; discuss with consultant
Reduce to 75% of previous dose; discuss with consultant

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg IV (30 min before methotrexate)
Dexamethasone 8mg IV (30 min before methotrexate)
Urinary alkalinisation: sodium bicarbonate IV (to achieve urine pH >7 before starting methotrexate; continue throughout infusion and until methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L)
Adequate IV hydration (>=3 litres/m²/day) before, during and after methotrexate
Furosemide 40mg IV or oral if urine output inadequate
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (antiviral prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (PCP prophylaxis)
special_warnings: CrCl >=50 ml/min is mandatory before each cycle; assess using EDTA GFR or Cockcroft-Gault formula
Urine pH must be >7 before methotrexate is commenced; do not start until pH confirmed
Serum methotrexate levels must be taken every 24 hours from 24h after the start of infusion until level <0.1 micromol/L
Folinic acid rescue (30mg every 3h IV) is mandatory and life-saving; must not be omitted
Delayed clearance may indicate renal toxicity; prolonged rescue and glucarpidase may be required — contact oncology urgently
Monitor fluid balance and urine output throughout; ensure >100ml/h urine output
This protocol requires inpatient administration
Many drugs interact with methotrexate (NSAIDs, penicillins, co-trimoxazole) — review concomitant medications

disease_site: Burkitt Lymphoma or High-Grade B-cell Lymphoma (patients aged <=65 years)

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%; administer according to local rituximab infusion guidelines
IV bolus over 10 minutes on day 1; vesicant — check line patency before administration; given after rituximab
IV bolus over 10 minutes in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9%; maximum single dose 2mg regardless of BSA; vesicant — fatal if given intrathecally; must be dispensed in a minibag
IV bolus over 10 minutes on day 1
IV bolus over 10 minutes on days 2-5; total cyclophosphamide dose per cycle 1600mg/m² (800 + 4x200)

INTRATHECAL administration — 70mg intrathecal on days 1 and 3; must be prescribed on an intrathecal chart in compliance with national guidance; this entry represents IT cytarabine as IV infusion equivalent for system purposes only; actual route is intrathecal; national and local intrathecal policies must be followed at all times

Loading dose 300mg/m² over 1 hour on day 10, immediately followed by 2700mg/m² maintenance infusion over 23 hours; total methotrexate dose day 10 is 3000mg/m²; urinary alkalinisation (urine pH >7) required before starting; CrCl >=50 required; folinic acid rescue commences 36h after start of MTX | LOADING DOSE (300mg/m²): two IV Methotrexate entries exist on day 10 — 300mg/m² loading infusion over 1 hour followed immediately by 2700mg/m² maintenance infusion over 23 hours. Total day 10 IV dose = 3000mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.

Maintenance infusion 2700mg/m² over 23 hours, given immediately after 300mg/m² loading dose; total methotrexate dose on day 10 is 3000mg/m²; serum methotrexate levels every 24h from 48h after start until <0.1 micromol/L; folinic acid rescue 30mg every 3h IV from 36h after start of MTX infusion until level <0.1 micromol/L | MAINTENANCE INFUSION DOSE (2700mg/m²): two IV Methotrexate entries exist on day 10 — 300mg/m² loading infusion over 1 hour followed immediately by this 2700mg/m² maintenance infusion over 23 hours. Total day 10 IV dose = 3000mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.
30mg every 3 hours IV starting 36 hours after the start of methotrexate infusion (day 10); continue until serum methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L; may switch to oral from day 13; mandatory rescue

INTRATHECAL administration — 12.5mg methotrexate intrathecal on day 15; must be prescribed on an intrathecal chart in compliance with national guidance; this entry represents IT methotrexate as IV infusion equivalent for system purposes only; actual route is intrathecal; national and local intrathecal policies must be followed at all times

Delay methotrexate until urine pH >=7; continue urinary alkalinisation
Reduce both to 50% dose
Omit both doxorubicin and vincristine; discuss with consultant

Hydrocortisone 100mg IV (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg IV twice daily (days 1-7)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral or IV twice daily (days 1-7)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily when required
Urinary alkalinisation: sodium bicarbonate IV to maintain urine pH >7 before and throughout day 10 methotrexate
Adequate IV hydration (>=3 litres/m²/day) around methotrexate

special_warnings: For patients aged <=65 years; use RCODOX-M66 protocol for patients aged >=66 years (lower methotrexate dose)
CrCl >=50 ml/min mandatory before each methotrexate administration (day 10)
Vincristine maximum single dose is 2mg regardless of BSA calculation

Vincristine is FATAL if given intrathecally — must be dispensed in minibag; intrathecal cytarabine and methotrexate must be administered on separate occasions by trained personnel following national intrathecal guidance
Intrathecal prescriptions must be written on a separate intrathecal chart in compliance with national guidance
Urinary alkalinisation (pH >7) is required before and throughout methotrexate infusion (day 10)
Serum methotrexate levels every 24h from 48h after start of infusion until <0.1 micromol/L
Folinic acid rescue 30mg every 3h IV must begin 36h after start of methotrexate
Tumour lysis syndrome risk in cycle 1: allopurinol and adequate hydration mandatory
Hodgkin Lymphoma or patients who have received blood products may carry TA-GVHD risk; if applicable ONLY irradiated blood products
This protocol may be used alternating with R-IVAC in high-risk patients (2 cycles each)

disease_site: Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) or other aggressive B-cell lymphoma

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%; administer according to local rituximab infusion guidelines on day 1 before continuous infusion drugs

50mg/m² per 24h continuous infusion bag (two 48h bags: days 1-2 and days 3-4); dose listed per 24h period; combined with doxorubicin and vincristine in same bag; total etoposide dose per cycle 100mg/m² x 2 bags = 200mg/m² over 96h total; requires PICC or central venous access; dose-adjusted based on nadir counts from previous cycle

10mg/m² per 24h continuous infusion bag (two 48h bags: days 1-2 and days 3-4); dose listed per 24h period; combined with etoposide and vincristine in same bag; total doxorubicin per cycle 10mg/m² x 2 bags = 20mg/m² x 2 = 40mg/m² over 96h; requires PICC or central venous access; dose-adjusted based on nadir counts from previous cycle

0.2mg/m² per 24h continuous infusion bag (two 48h bags: days 1-2 and days 3-4); dose listed per 24h period; combined with etoposide and doxorubicin in same bag; total vincristine per cycle 0.2mg/m² x 2 bags = 0.4mg/m² x 2 = 0.8mg/m² over 96h; maximum dose per bag is capped at 2mg; NOT dose-adjusted (vincristine dose remains fixed); requires PICC or central venous access
IV bolus over 10 minutes on day 5 (after completion of 96h continuous infusion); dose-adjusted based on nadir counts from previous cycle
100mg oral once daily on days 1-5; not dose-adjusted

dose_modifications.condition: Cycle 1 starting doses (no prior nadir data)
dose_modifications.notes: Start at standard doses: Etoposide 50mg/m²/24h, Doxorubicin 10mg/m²/24h, Cyclophosphamide 750mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Nadir neutrophils >=0.5 x10⁹/L AND nadir platelets >=50 x10⁹/L in previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Escalate dose by 20% above previous cycle dose for all three drugs (dose escalation). DA-R-EPOCH dose escalation: increase etoposide, doxorubicin, and cyclophosphamide by 20% each cycle if nadir ANC ≥0.5 and nadir platelets ≥50. Dose escalation is NOT represented as a factor — managed by clinical team at each cycle review.

dose_modifications.condition: Nadir neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L on 1-2 occasions OR nadir platelets <50 x10⁹/L on 1-2 occasions in previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Maintain same dose as previous cycle (no change)

dose_modifications.condition: Nadir neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L on 3 or more occasions OR nadir platelets <50 x10⁹/L on 3 or more occasions in previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 20% below previous cycle dose for all three drugs (dose de-escalation)
Reduce cyclophosphamide by 25%; discuss with consultant

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin 26-50 micromol/L or ALT/AST 2-5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce doxorubicin and etoposide to 50%

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin >50 micromol/L or ALT/AST >5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Omit doxorubicin and etoposide; discuss with consultant
Reduce vincristine to 75%
Omit vincristine from continuous infusion bag

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (pre-rituximab, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg IV or oral twice daily (days 1-5)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times daily when required
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (antiviral prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral Monday/Wednesday/Friday (PCP prophylaxis)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily (cycle 1 only, tumour lysis prophylaxis)
G-CSF: filgrastim or lenograstim from day 6 until neutrophil count >1.0x10⁹/L (per local protocol)
special_warnings: DA-R-EPOCH is DOSE-ADJUSTED: dose escalation (20% per cycle) and de-escalation rules apply based on nadir counts. This requires per-cycle clinical review — doses are NOT fixed.
Dose adjustment (escalation or de-escalation) applies to etoposide, doxorubicin, and cyclophosphamide only — vincristine and prednisolone doses are fixed
Vincristine maximum dose per 48h bag is 2mg; do not exceed regardless of BSA
PICC line or central venous access is mandatory for the 96h continuous infusion; peripheral IV access is not acceptable
The 96h infusion is delivered as two separate 48h bags (bag 1 days 1-2, bag 2 days 3-4) each containing etoposide, doxorubicin, and vincristine
Cyclophosphamide is given as an IV bolus on day 5, after the infusion is complete
Nadir blood counts must be checked between days 10-15 of each cycle to determine dose adjustment for the next cycle
G-CSF support is required from day 6 to support dose-escalation strategy
Doxorubicin is a vesicant; given as continuous infusion — central access mandatory to avoid extravasation
Tumour lysis syndrome risk in cycle 1: allopurinol and adequate hydration mandatory

Anti-CD20 therapy: HBV reactivation risk — screen all patients for HBsAg and HBcAb before starting. HBsAg positive: antiviral prophylaxis mandatory (entecavir or tenofovir). HBcAb positive/HBsAg negative: prophylaxis or close monitoring per local hepatology policy.
